title: Monitoring / alerting configuration inventory
description: One record per monitor / alert rule across a monitoring vendor (Datadog monitor, Grafana alert rule): the rule's name, type, enabled state, and the NAMES/HANDLES of the notification targets it routes to. Pulled read-only from the vendor API (Datadog monitors_read; Grafana Viewer service-account). Configuration + routing-target metadata only — never secret webhook URLs, integration tokens, recipient email addresses (PII), dashboard JSON, metric time-series, or log query results. The connector reports a descriptive enabled/disabled posture; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope). Shared across the Datadog + Grafana connectors because the two vendors' alert-configuration field set is identical at the config-inventory altitude; the source_vendor field preserves provenance.

source_vendor.description: Monitoring vendor the record was collected from.

rule_id.description: Vendor-native stable identifier of the monitor / alert rule (non-secret; an opaque ID, not a credential).

rule_name.description: Human-readable name of the monitor / alert rule.

rule_type.description: Vendor-native rule type (e.g. Datadog 'metric alert' / 'log alert'; Grafana 'grafana' / 'datasource' rule). Descriptive string, not enumerated, so new vendor types do not require a schema bump.

enabled.description: Whether the monitor / alert rule is currently active (not muted / paused / disabled).

folder.description: Optional organizational grouping (Grafana folder; absent for Datadog). Non-secret label.

notification_targets.description: The notification targets the rule routes to — NAMES / HANDLES only, never secret webhook URLs, tokens, or recipient email addresses.

target_kind.description: Kind of notification target (e.g. 'slack', 'pagerduty', 'email', 'webhook', 'contact_point'). The KIND, not the secret.

target_name.description: The handle / channel name / contact-point name of the target (e.g. '#sec-oncall', 'pd-primary', 'Default contact point'). NEVER the secret webhook URL, integration token, or a recipient email address.
